sections.paragraph:
an unused root; prop. i.q. כָּתַת to beat, to pound; hence-

(1) to strike fire, whence כִּידֹוד a spark, and כַּדְכֹּד a sparkling gem, a ruby.

(2) to labour heavily, toilsomely, like smiths (comp. cudo); specially to draw water from a well. Hence is כַּד. (Arab. كَدَّ to pound, to labour toilsomely, to draw from a well, كَيْدُ a striking fire: [“compare Eth. ከደደ፡”]).
